<!-- Mirrored from ~/.claude/commands/recenter.md by scripts/harness/sync-commands-to-prompts.sh -- do not edit directly -->
---
description: Re-anchor to the standing rules — name which ones you broke in this conversation and correct course.
---

You've likely drifted from the standing rules over the course of this conversation (rules decay as context grows — that's why I'm invoking this). Stop and re-center:

1. Re-read the active rule surfaces: `~/.claude/rules/communication-style.md`, `~/.claude/rules/learned-rules.md`, and the current workspace's `CLAUDE.md` + `.claude/rules/`.
2. Review the recent turns of THIS conversation. Identify the specific rules you broke — name each one and quote the exact moment you broke it. Do not speak in generalities ("I'll be more concise"); cite the actual violation and the rule it violated.
3. If you genuinely can't find a concrete violation, say so in one line rather than inventing one to look responsive.
4. Correct course now: redo or amend the offending response, honoring the rules.

Be blunt and brief. No groveling, no over-apology — acknowledge, name, fix, move on.

$ARGUMENTS
